name: Tofu Crab Cakes (Yan)
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Japanese soup stock or chicken stock
1/3 cup sweet cooking rice wine or mirin
1/2 cup Japanese-style bread crumbs or panko
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak dried black mushrooms in warm water to cover until softened, about 20 minutes. Drain and discard stems, trim caps, and finely dice them.
2. Drain tofu and crumble by hand. Let drain for 15 minutes, then wrap in a clean dish towel and twist to extract moisture. Add tofu to the diced mushrooms.
3. Mix crabmeat, shrimp, green onions, egg white, ginger, sugar, and salt into the mixture. Shape into ten cakes about 1/2-inch thick.
4. In a small pan, combine dashi, mirin, soy sauce, sugar, and ginger. Simmer for 3 minutes. Add cornstarch solution and cook until boiling and thickened. Keep warm.
5. Beat remaining egg yolk with water. Dip crab cakes in cornstarch, shake off excess, then coat with egg wash. Finally, coat with bread crumbs.
6. Heat oil to a depth of 1/4 inch in a frying pan over medium heat. Add crab cakes and fry until golden brown, turning once, about 5-6 minutes per side. Remove and drain on paper towels.
7. Place cakes on a serving platter. Pour sauce over, garnish with green onion and sesame seeds.
Makes 10 crab cakes.
